title: Physocarpus tanakae
taxonomy: Physocarpus tanakae is classified within the family Rosaceae, commonly known as the rose family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants. Within the Rosaceae family, Physocarpus tanakae is a member of the genus Physocarpus, which consists of several species of ninebark shrubs. The genus Physocarpus is further classified within the order Rosales and the class Magnoliopsida. Overall, Physocarpus tanakae's classification reflects its botanical relationships with other flowering plants.

intro: Physocarpus tanakae, also known as Tanakae ninebark, is a plant species native to East Asia, characterized by its distinctive nine-lobed leaves and pinkish-white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Tanakae ninebark include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional East Asian herbal medicine, Tanakae ninebark is often prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures to leverage its health benefits.
